Title: Overexpression of Anthocyanidin Reductase Increases Flavonoids Content to Combat Fusarium Wilt in the Root Xylem of Vernicia montana

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of (-)-epigallocatechin from cyanidin in vitro; study also places it directly in biosynthesis of (-)-epicatechin and (-)-epigallocatechin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control gene for flavonoid accumulation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: (-)-Epigallocatechin; (-)-Epicatechin; Catechin; Myricitrin; Phlorizin; Naringenin; Dihydromyricetin; Luteolin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial defense against Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. fordiis; catechin, (-)-epicatechin and (-)-epigallocatechin inhibit fungal growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases multiple flavonoids and is associated with enhanced resistance to Fusarium wilt; silencing reduces catechin and myricitrin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated transcriptomics-metabolomics correlation; RT-qPCR; Agrobacterium rhizogenes-mediated hairy-root overexpression; VIGS silencing; HPLC-MS metabolite profiling; recombinant protein enzyme assay in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated after Fof-1 infection in V. montana root xylem at all tested stages (2, 5, 9 dpi)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in infected root xylem; ~35-fold increased in OE hairy roots; down-regulated in VIGS leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. fordiis infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering Fusarium wilt-resistant tung oil trees by boosting antimicrobial flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmANR (A008279) was consistently induced in resistant V. montana root xylem after Fof-1 infection. 2. VmANR expression positively correlated with accumulation of multiple flavonoids. 3. Overexpression in hairy roots elevated eight flavonoids, while VIGS silencing reduced catechin and myricitrin. 4. Recombinant VmANR catalyzed formation of (-)-epigallocatechin from cyanidin in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A second ANR-like locus A008266 was identified, but its conserved domain was incomplete, so A008279 was chosen for functional validation. The paper reports a novel reaction: formation of (-)-epigallocatechin from cyanidin by VmANR.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Annotated ANR gene directly involved in biosynthesis of (-)-epicatechin and (-)-epigallocatechin based on pathway mapping
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial defense against Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. fordiis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive association with antimicrobial flavonoid accumulation after infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated after Fof-1 infection in V. montana root xylem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. fordiis infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate ANR locus for flavonoid engineering, but caution due to incomplete conserved domain
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A008266 was one of two ANR genes induced by Fof-1 infection in V. montana. 2. It showed positive correlation with many flavonoids. 3. Functional validation was not pursued because its conserved domain was incomplete.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Conserved domain judged incomplete by NCBI search; not selected for functional verification.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes chalcone to flavanone in the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 13 infection-induced flavonoids including catechin, (-)-epicatechin, (-)-epigallocatechin, naringenin and others
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports antimicrobial flavonoid accumulation during defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulated with flavonoid accumulation after Fof-1 infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in infected V. montana root xylem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. fordiis infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upstream engineering target for boosting overall flavonoid flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI (A005627) was significantly up-regulated after infection. 2. It was identified as a key metabolic enzyme contributing to biosynthesis of the induced flavonoid set. 3. No direct functional manipulation was performed in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Annotated flavonoid-pathway P450 involved in biosynthesis of infection-induced flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool or branch modification toward target flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Infection-induced flavonoids including dihydromyricetin, myricetin and related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports antimicrobial flavonoid accumulation during defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly up-regulated after Fof-1 infection and associated with flavonoid biosynthesis activation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in infected V. montana root xylem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. fordiis infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway gene for combinatorial flavonoid engineering with VmANR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP75B1 (A021985) was among the infection-induced flavonoid biosynthesis genes. 2. It was proposed to participate in synthesis of the 13 induced flavonoid metabolites. 3. No direct functional validation was reported in this study.
